asset_family: fishing gameplay fake shot and reusable UI source families
source_family_role: non-stretch ornaments, gems, badges, caps, medallions with anchors

recommended_canvas.background_policy: perfectly flat chroma or true transparency only

rows.purpose: corner caps, edge caps, dividers, glow strips, ornamental bars
rows.item_policy: non-stretch overlays only; obvious anchor point; transparent/chroma padding around every sprite

rows.purpose: badges, gems, locks, seals, medallions, plaques, banners
rows.item_policy: each ornament isolated as its own sprite; never baked into panel centers

prompt: Create a production source sheet for fishing gameplay fake shot and reusable UI source families. Source family: ui decor overlay sheet. Role: non-stretch ornaments, gems, badges, caps, medallions with anchors. This is a cuttable source sheet, not a gameplay screenshot, mockup, landing page, or composed UI screen. Arrange the sheet as a row_major_grid with at least 64px outer margin and 64px gutters between all visible pixels and shadows. Row 1 corner_and_edge_overlays: corner caps, edge caps, dividers, glow strips, ornamental bars; non-stretch overlays only; obvious anchor point; transparent/chroma padding around every sprite. Row 2 badges_and_fixed_ornaments: badges, gems, locks, seals, medallions, plaques, banners; each ornament isolated as its own sprite; never baked into panel centers. Leave empty chroma lanes between rows and slots. Do not compose these assets into a runtime screen. Use a perfectly flat chroma background #00ff00 or true transparency; do not use gradients, shadows, glow, or texture in the background. Keep every asset isolated with generous gutters and no overlap between shadows. Keep all labels, numbers, quest text, counters, and state values blank for runtime composition. Create only non-stretch decorative overlays: corner caps, badges, gems, medallions, locks, banners, dividers, and ornamental flourishes with clear transparent/chroma padding and anchorable silhouettes. Put unique gems, badges, medallions, locks, banners, and center ornaments in separate overlay sprites, not inside stretchable bases. Use consistent perspective, lighting, material language, and line weight across the sheet.
negative_prompt: button labels, resource counters, timer values, tutorial text, debug text, game state values, random letters, watermarks, labels fused into reusable backgrounds, icons fused into buttons, wrong subject, weak silhouette at gameplay size, opaque chroma-key background, baked board state, readable text, fake letters, watermark, fused icons inside buttons, cropped silhouettes, merged components, busy stretch centers, non-flat chroma background, purple or red-blue halo on transparent edges
constraints: no fused icons inside button backgrounds
transparent or flat chroma-key background for isolated assets
no unique decoration inside slice9 stretch zones

final_asset_policy.notes: Code may cut, validate, pack, and compose. Code-generated/programmer art is not acceptable as final generated UI unless an explicit exception is recorded.

acceptance_checklist: Source sheet is not a full gameplay screenshot.
Background is flat #00ff00 or true transparent alpha.
No readable text, fake glyphs, labels, numbers, or watermark.
Components have clear gutters and do not share antialias/shadow pixels.
No stretchable slice9 bases are mixed into this source family.
Every decorative overlay is isolated as a separate sprite with padding and obvious anchor point.
Icons/sprites have full silhouettes with padding for alpha trim.
Source can pass source-sheet intake before crop rectangles are trusted.
Accepted output will get a generation record with provider/model/workflow/seed or no-seed reason.
